question.text: An applicant incorporated or established in an International Financial Services Centre (IFSC) is deemed to be:

answer.text: Ineligible for FPI registration in India
Appropriately regulated for FPI registration purposes
Required to obtain separate SEBI registration before FPI application
Eligible only for Category-II FPI registration

explanation.text: **The Logic:** As per SEBI (FPI) Regulations, 2019, an applicant incorporated or established in an International Financial Services Centre shall be deemed to be appropriately regulated. This means IFSC-based entities do not need to separately demonstrate regulatory compliance from their home jurisdiction. **The Trap:** IFSC-based entities enjoy several relaxations including exemption from certain NRI/OCI contribution limits and deemed regulatory status. Candidates may not be aware of these special provisions. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** IFSC entities regulated by IFSCA benefit from a special dispensation under FPI regulations, reflecting India's push to develop Gujarat's GIFT IFSC as an international financial hub.
